current_status_update.title: Status update - Dec 12

current_status_update.text:
Completed a review of v3 diff and existing Nylas features that we have not leveraged

https://developer.nylas.com/docs/v2/upgrade-to-v3/diff-view/#terminology-changes-in-v3

A few interesting new features which would require additional dev:

Email
    New webhook event for emails message.opened could be used to indicate that an internal user had seen a message

Calendars
    Check a calendar for free/busy status
    Each grant can now have up to 10 virtual calendars + Added the option to specify a primary calendar
    ”You can now send drafts” - save?
    You can schedule a send time for a message, and edit or delete scheduled send times. - The new message.send_success and message.send_failed notifications allow you to track the results of a scheduled send
    The new message.bounce_detected notification is available to check for message bounces from Google, Microsoft Graph, iCloud, and Yahoo.
    You can now soft-delete messages and threads

Also discussed Nylas’ scheduling tools which could address some user requests - after chatting with @Jordan Jan and Allie, it seems that this integration would be a heavy lift and wouldn’t qualify for low-hanging fruit
    https://developer.nylas.com/docs/v3/calendar/group-booking/
current_status_update.title: Reviewed v3 diff - no low-hanging fruit, but a few new interesting features to consider

current_status_update.text:
Summary
We are putting intake to case syncing on hold. After further discovery, we learned that in order for us to make a meaningful impact on where CX would feel comfortable in recommending that more customers use Intake Reports, we needed to include notes syncing at a bare minimum. This cannot be completed within the quarter. We've decided to reallocate that time to further reinforcing our notes auto-save work and will investigate ways in which we might be able to provide more functionality than what we planned to do on that initiative.

We'll reconsider this initiative in context of other 2025 initiatives.

current_status_update.text:
Summary
Initial investigation focused on a potential data leak related to the use of the Form.io Scala proxy. We believed this might affect both form templates and submission data. However, after further review, we confirmed that the issue is limited to metadata within the form templates only — no sensitive or submission data is exposed.

With this clarification, we’ve downgraded the risk to low and decided no immediate action is required at this time.

current_status_update.title: GP - People (Limited Profile) - Aug 13

current_status_update.text:
Summary
The original concern was that users could gain unsanctioned visibility into other people receiving services via shared service notes — particularly when those notes appeared on units of work the user had access to, even if the other people referenced in the note were from different cases or intakes.
This concern has now been resolved through enhancements delivered as part of adjacent initiatives:

What’s been addressed:
    Optional Service Note Linking:
        Users now only see and can associate notes with units of work they have access to. This removes the possibility of inadvertently surfacing service notes that reference people on other UoWs the user shouldn’t see.
    System Preference Page:
        Admins can configure whether note associations default to all eligible enrollments or just the current UoW, reducing the risk of cross-UoW note sharing by default.
    Clarified Platform Permission Model:
        A platform-wide decision was made that access to a unit of work implies access to the people on that unit of work, resolving ambiguity about expected visibility patterns and helping standardize behavior across applications.

Summary:
Group Note Visibility issues have been effectively handled through work completed on Optional Service Note Linking, System Preferences, and platform-wide permissions alignment. No further dedicated development is required at this time.

Next steps

current_status_update.text:
Summary
We have a plan for moving forward. 
    DE - Implement a new implementation strategy for the People Table. [1]
        Reduce query complexity and do partial re-compute
    DE - Converting view to tables
        Chunk into groups of 10 most important/used/heavy/slow views
        Chunk 1:
            case_involvements
            service_enrollments
            cases
            service_note_recipients
            intake
            case_involvements_recipients
            notes
            service_notes
            case_involvements_point_in_time
            services
        We will evaluate after this chunk to see if we move forward with more. 

Unrelated but close:
    BE - Create a new field for Case Inactive Date
    DE - Stop versioning redshift tables
    DE - VARCHAR(MAX) - DATA-2474
    BE/DE - GQL Chattiness
    SPIKE - Kafka ‘data loss’ issues


Next steps

current_status_update.title: Dynamic Headers in WLVs - Feb 26

current_status_update.text:
Summary
Done and gonna use them today! We added:
    A workflow template view that allows me to evaluate not just workflow instances but how folks are setting up their templates
    A tenants view with all tenants and the info from the db as well as basic chargebee data. 
    A users and people view with all users and people in casebook
    cbp_form_submissions with all data from all form_submission (cases, intake, people, providers) and the fields

And we updated: 
    cbp_dashboard_field_metadata with:
        datasource type (Casebook datasets versus form datasets)
        dashboard_creation_date 
        dashborad_last_updated
    cbp_dashboard_join_metadata:
        primary_datasource_type - contains value Casebook if the primary_datasource field is from a reporting view and Form if the primary_datasource field is a Formio form table
        secondary_datasource_type - contains value Casebook if the secondary_datasource field is from a reporting view and Form if the secondary_datasource field is a Formio form table
        dashboard_creation_date 
        dashborad_last_updated


Next steps
